eval_mechanism: prime eval run (deployed adapter gjgpwgdb5sh7y2d6epmveh23, base Qwen3.5-2B)

shared_config.note_episode_count: Eval split has 128 seeds; each cell rolled out 64 episodes (run logs say '64 groups, 64 total rollouts'). Reported means/std are over these 64. SEM = std/sqrt(64). The paper uses 64.

headline_findings: Gap widens at scale: lift over untrained goes from 21x mean at 50 leads to 50x at 100 leads.
Untrained drops 4.3x as leads double (1.3% -> 0.3%); trained drops 1.8x on average.
Trained model holds 7.9-19.7% conv/lead at 100 leads across 4 unseen buyer variants (no overfit to default buyer).

Skeptical is the hardest buyer: lowest absolute conversion (7.9% at 100 leads) and steepest reward collapse (0.133 -> 0.017). Its per-lead conversion drops ~2.0x (0.160 -> 0.079), comparable to the other styles (1.5-2.2x), but still ~26x over untrained. (Earlier '5x drop' was an error.)
Context summarization is essential at 100 leads: 8.5 summaries/episode trained-default; without summarization, 47% of episodes errored on context overflow.
